channel.title: International Relations on MinWeb
channel.description: Recent content in International Relations on MinWeb

item.title: Pentagon Planning Possible Military Operation in Cuba
item.description:
If you feel like you’ve been living in a state of perpetual whiplash lately, you aren’t alone. Just when it seems like the headlines couldn’t get any more volatile, Washington has shifted its focus to a new, explosive target: Cuba.

If you’re frustrated, tired, or just plain confused about why the administration is setting its sights on Havana while other conflicts remain unresolved, you’re in the right place. Honestly, it feels like the government is trying to juggle too many chainsaws at once. Let’s strip away the political rhetoric and look at what is actually happening in the Caribbean, the real risks involved, and why this is suddenly dominating the Pentagon’s to-do list.

item.title: Trump’s Pearl Harbor Joke: The Diplomatic Cost of a Quip
item.description:
If you have ever had a boss or colleague kill a deal by making an inappropriate joke at the worst possible moment, you understand exactly why the U.S.-Japan relationship is currently hitting a wall. On March 19, 2026, President Donald Trump turned a high-stakes diplomatic meeting into a public embarrassment by joking about Pearl Harbor to the Japanese Prime Minister.

_IMG_PLACEHOLDER

Diplomacy is about trust, and trust is built on consistency. When a world leader treats a historic trauma like a casual quip, they lose more than a punchline. They lose the leverage needed to secure military and economic cooperation.
